description: Corner-bracket realism expansion for the L-bracket benchmark with one added stiffness-dominated Group E task.

coordinate_system.x: primary depth direction measured outward from the corner bisector or mounting reference as defined per task

global_conventions.coordinate_system_note: For Group E tasks, explicit envelope_ranges_mm and interface coordinates are the source of truth. The coordinate frame is task-specific but always expressed in the same global x/y/z format.

submission_requirements.assembly_scope: single bracket body unless task explicitly allows multi-part welded assembly

evaluation_method.constraints_policy: all load cases must satisfy all constraints
evaluation_method.displacement_metric: maximum displacement magnitude at load interface
evaluation_method.mass_definition: Bracket-body mass only. Fasteners and mating components are excluded.

envelope_scope.definition: The task envelope constrains only the bracket geometry. The supporting walls, frames, or attached components are excluded from the envelope.

load_application_rule.definition: Each load case is applied as a distributed load over the specified load interface patch.

minimum_edge_distance_definition.definition: minimum_edge_distance_mm is the minimum Euclidean distance from the center of any mounting hole to the nearest free edge of the bracket body.

evaluation_mesh_specification_template.stress_extraction_rule: Report peak von Mises stress away from immediate singular boundary-condition artifacts.

load_interface_placement_rationale.definition: For corner-bracket tasks, the primary load interface should lie on or immediately adjacent to one bracket leg or one reinforced face, rather than at a purely diagonal point in free space, unless a task explicitly states a diagonal bridging interface for a justified use case.

slot_boundary_condition_rule.definition: For slot-based mounting tasks in v1.2.1, the entire explicit slot surface is constrained in translation for reproducibility. Bearing-direction-dependent contact is intentionally not modeled at this stage.

wraparound_compliance_rule.definition: Wraparound compliance is checked geometrically by evaluating occupancy within the specified wraparound region on evenly spaced z-slices. A slice counts as occupied if bracket material covers at least the stated minimum fraction of the region area on that slice.

load_interface_dimension_axis_rule.definition: The size_mm keys for a load_interface refer to the global coordinate axes that lie in the plane of that specific load interface. Therefore different tasks may use different axis pairs, such as {y, z}, {x, z}, or {x, y}, depending on the orientation of the load pad.

wraparound_occupancy_material_rule.definition: Wraparound occupancy is computed using actual remaining bracket material on each evaluation slice. Holes, slots, voids, pockets, and cutouts reduce occupied area and are not ignored in the occupancy calculation.

slot_edge_distance_rule.definition: If a task explicitly states that minimum_edge_distance_mm for a slot is measured from the slot centroid, that centroid-based rule is intentional and takes precedence over perimeter-based interpretations.

group_E_difficulty_balance_note.definition: Group E now includes one explicitly stiffness-dominated task intended to broaden the governing-mechanism coverage beyond primarily strength-governed corner-bracket cases.

tasks.name: Inside-corner bracket, round holes
tasks.design_requirements_nl: Design an inside-corner reinforcement bracket for joining two perpendicular mounting surfaces at an internal 90 degree corner. The bracket is mounted across the corner and attaches to both intersecting planes. Use a coordinate system in which the corner line is parallel to z, the two mounting planes are x = 0 and y = 0, and the bracket occupies the positive-x positive-y region near the corner. The bracket body must fit inside an axis-aligned bounding box with x in [0, 70] mm, y in [0, 70] mm, and z in [-45, 45] mm. The mounting interfaces are two perpendicular planes meeting at the corner. The x = 0 plane contains two 6.6 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (0, 18, -22) mm and (0, 18, 22) mm. The y = 0 plane contains two 6.6 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (18, 0, -22) mm and (18, 0, 22) mm. The primary load interface is a flat rectangular pad located on the x-dominant leg region, centered at (52, 10, 0) mm with size 22 mm in x by 20 mm in z. Apply each service load as a distributed load over the load pad with equivalent resultant passing through the load pad center. The bracket must withstand four static load cases: 1200 N downward in negative z, 1200 N upward in positive z, 700 N in positive x, and 500 N in positive y. Use ASTM A36 steel with elastic modulus 200 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.29, yield strength 250 MPa, and density 7850 kg/m^3. Assume machined single-body manufacturing with minimum thickness 5 mm, minimum internal fillet radius 3 mm, and no undercuts. minimum_edge_distance_mm is defined as the minimum distance from hole center to nearest free edge. The objective is to minimize bracket mass while keeping maximum displacement at the load interface below 1.0 mm and maximum von Mises stress below 83.3 MPa under every load case.

evaluation_assumptions.mounting_boundaries: constrained at cylindrical hole surfaces on both mounting planes

reference_solution.notes: Reference geometry concept attached in v1.2.4. CAD/FEA validation results are still pending.

geometry_concept.notes: This is the first reference-geometry concept for E-T1-01. It is intended as a conservative baseline for feasibility validation, not as a mass-optimized design.
geometry_concept.expected_role: first CAD/FEA validation baseline for dual-plane boundary conditions and load-interface interpretation
geometry_concept.cadquery_code:
import cadquery as cq

# E-T1-01 reference geometry
# Units: mm

# -----------------------------
# Parameters
# -----------------------------
t = 5.0           # leg thickness
Lx = 60.0         # x-leg length
Ly = 60.0         # y-leg length
H = 64.0          # overall height in z
r_root = 3.0      # internal root fillet radius

hole_d = 6.6
z_hole = 22.0
offset_face = 18.0

# z-range is [-H/2, H/2]
z0 = -H / 2.0

# -----------------------------
# Build two perpendicular legs
# -----------------------------
# Leg A: x in [0, Lx], y in [0, t], z in [-H/2, H/2]
leg_x = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(Lx, t, H, centered=(False, False, True))
)

# Leg B: x in [0, t], y in [0, Ly], z in [-H/2, H/2]
leg_y = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(t, Ly, H, centered=(False, False, True))
)

part = leg_x.union(leg_y)

# -----------------------------
# Fillet the internal re-entrant vertical edge
# -----------------------------
# The inner edge is the vertical line near (x=t, y=t)
vertical_edges = part.edges("|Z")
part = vertical_edges.nearestTo((t, t, 0)).fillet(r_root)

# -----------------------------
# Holes on x=0 face
# Centers: (0, 18, -22), (0, 18, 22)
# -----------------------------
part = (
    part.faces("<X")
    .workplane()
    .center(offset_face, -z_hole)
    .hole(hole_d)
)

part = (
    part.faces("<X")
    .workplane()
    .center(offset_face, z_hole)
    .hole(hole_d)
)

# -----------------------------
# Holes on y=0 face
# Centers: (18, 0, -22), (18, 0, 22)
# -----------------------------
part = (
    part.faces("<Y")
    .workplane()
    .center(offset_face, -z_hole)
    .hole(hole_d)
)

part = (
    part.faces("<Y")
    .workplane()
    .center(offset_face, z_hole)
    .hole(hole_d)
)

# Export if needed:
# cq.exporters.export(part, "E_T1_01_reference.step")

show_object(part)

feasibility_check.notes: Reference geometry concept is attached. Next step is mesh generation and four-load-case validation.

tasks.name: Outside-corner bracket
tasks.design_requirements_nl: Design an outside-corner bracket for reinforcing an external 90 degree corner. The bracket attaches across two perpendicular exterior faces and must bridge the outside of the corner. Use a coordinate system in which the corner line is parallel to z, the two reference mounting planes are x = 0 and y = 0, and the bracket body occupies the negative-x negative-y region outside the corner. The bracket body must fit inside an axis-aligned bounding box with x in [-75, 0] mm, y in [-75, 0] mm, and z in [-45, 45] mm. The x = 0 plane contains two 6.6 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (0, -20, -24) mm and (0, -20, 24) mm. The y = 0 plane contains two 6.6 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (-20, 0, -24) mm and (-20, 0, 24) mm. The primary load interface is a flat rectangular pad located on the x-side leg region, centered at (-56, -10, 0) mm with size 22 mm in x by 20 mm in z. Apply each service load as a distributed load over the load pad with equivalent resultant passing through the load pad center. The bracket must withstand four static load cases: 1300 N downward in negative z, 1300 N upward in positive z, 750 N in negative x, and 550 N in negative y. Use stainless steel 304 with elastic modulus 193 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.29, yield strength 215 MPa, and density 8000 kg/m^3. Assume machined single-body manufacturing with minimum thickness 5 mm, minimum internal fillet radius 3 mm, and no undercuts. minimum_edge_distance_mm is defined as the minimum distance from hole center to nearest free edge. The objective is to minimize bracket mass while keeping maximum displacement at the load interface below 0.9 mm and maximum von Mises stress below 71.7 MPa under every load case.

evaluation_assumptions.mounting_boundaries: constrained at cylindrical hole surfaces on both mounting planes

reference_solution.notes: Reference solution not yet attached in v1.2.4. Add a known-feasible baseline CAD/FEA design in a future revision.

feasibility_check.notes: Quick baseline CAD/FEA feasibility check should be attached in a future revision.

tasks.name: Wraparound corner bracket
tasks.design_requirements_nl: Design a wraparound corner bracket for reinforcing an internal 90 degree corner. The bracket must engage both mounting planes and also include a wraparound geometry that extends around the corner region to increase connection stiffness. Use a coordinate system in which the corner line is parallel to z, the mounting planes are x = 0 and y = 0, and the bracket body occupies the positive-x positive-y region near the corner. The bracket body must fit inside an axis-aligned bounding box with x in [0, 85] mm, y in [0, 85] mm, and z in [-50, 50] mm. The wraparound feature is a geometric requirement: the bracket must include material in the region x in [0, 12] mm and y in [0, 12] mm. Wraparound compliance is checked on 10 evenly spaced z-slices across the full z span. A slice counts as compliant if actual remaining bracket material occupies at least 50 percent of the rectangular wraparound region area on that slice; holes, slots, voids, pockets, and cutouts reduce the occupied area and are not ignored. At least 7 of the 10 slices must be compliant. The x = 0 plane contains two 8.5 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (0, 22, -26) mm and (0, 22, 26) mm. The y = 0 plane contains two 8.5 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (22, 0, -26) mm and (22, 0, 26) mm. The primary load interface is a flat rectangular pad located on the x-dominant reinforced leg region, centered at (62, 12, 0) mm with size 24 mm in x by 22 mm in z. Apply each service load as a distributed load over the load pad with equivalent resultant passing through the load pad center. The bracket must withstand four static load cases: 1700 N downward in negative z, 1700 N upward in positive z, 900 N in positive x, and 900 N in positive y. Use ASTM A36 steel with elastic modulus 200 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.29, yield strength 250 MPa, and density 7850 kg/m^3. Assume welded fabrication with at most 3 structural pieces, minimum plate thickness 6 mm, minimum weld leg size 5 mm, and no undercuts. minimum_edge_distance_mm is defined as the minimum distance from hole center to nearest free edge. The objective is to minimize bracket mass while keeping maximum displacement at the load interface below 0.8 mm and maximum von Mises stress below 83.3 MPa under every load case.

evaluation_assumptions.mounting_boundaries: constrained at cylindrical hole surfaces on both mounting planes

reference_solution.notes: Reference solution not yet attached in v1.2.4. Add a known-feasible baseline CAD/FEA design in a future revision.

feasibility_check.notes: Quick baseline CAD/FEA feasibility check should be attached in a future revision.

tasks.name: Inside-corner bracket with slotted holes
tasks.design_requirements_nl: Design an inside-corner bracket for joining two perpendicular mounting surfaces at an internal 90 degree corner, using slotted mounting holes to permit installation tolerance. Use a coordinate system in which the corner line is parallel to z, the two mounting planes are x = 0 and y = 0, and the bracket body occupies the positive-x positive-y region near the corner. The bracket body must fit inside an axis-aligned bounding box with x in [0, 72] mm, y in [0, 72] mm, and z in [-45, 45] mm. The x = 0 mounting plane contains two slotted holes centered at (0, 18, -22) mm and (0, 18, 22) mm. Each slot must have width 6.6 mm, length 14 mm, and its long axis parallel to z. The y = 0 mounting plane contains two slotted holes centered at (18, 0, -22) mm and (18, 0, 22) mm. Each slot must have width 6.6 mm, length 14 mm, and its long axis parallel to z. Slot geometry must be modeled explicitly in geometry and finite-element evaluation. For reproducibility, the entire slot surface is constrained in translation for all load cases; bearing-direction-dependent contact is intentionally not modeled at this stage. The slot-end radius is therefore explicitly 3.3 mm. The primary load interface is a flat rectangular pad located on the x-dominant leg region, centered at (54, 10, 0) mm with size 20 mm in x by 20 mm in z. Apply each service load as a distributed load over the load pad with equivalent resultant passing through the load pad center. The bracket must withstand four static load cases: 1150 N downward in negative z, 1150 N upward in positive z, 650 N in positive x, and 500 N in positive y. Use stainless steel 304 with elastic modulus 193 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.29, yield strength 215 MPa, and density 8000 kg/m^3. Assume machined single-body manufacturing with minimum thickness 5 mm, minimum internal radius at slot ends 3.3 mm, and no undercuts. minimum_edge_distance_mm is intentionally defined as the minimum distance from slot center to nearest free edge using the slot centroid as the reference point, not the slot perimeter. The objective is to minimize bracket mass while keeping maximum displacement at the load interface below 0.95 mm and maximum von Mises stress below 71.7 MPa under every load case.

evaluation_assumptions.mounting_boundaries: the entire explicit slot surface is constrained in translation on both mounting planes

reference_solution.notes: Reference solution not yet attached in v1.2.4. Add a known-feasible baseline CAD/FEA design in a future revision.

feasibility_check.notes: Quick baseline CAD/FEA feasibility check should be attached in a future revision.

tasks.name: Inside-corner aluminum bracket, stiffness-dominated
tasks.design_requirements_nl: Design an inside-corner reinforcement bracket for joining two perpendicular mounting surfaces at an internal 90 degree corner, with the task intentionally made stiffness-dominated rather than primarily strength-dominated. The bracket is mounted across the corner and attaches to both intersecting planes. Use a coordinate system in which the corner line is parallel to z, the two mounting planes are x = 0 and y = 0, and the bracket occupies the positive-x positive-y region near the corner. The bracket body must fit inside an axis-aligned bounding box with x in [0, 82] mm, y in [0, 82] mm, and z in [-48, 48] mm. The mounting interfaces are two perpendicular planes meeting at the corner. The x = 0 plane contains two 6.6 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (0, 20, -24) mm and (0, 20, 24) mm. The y = 0 plane contains two 6.6 mm diameter mounting holes centered at (20, 0, -24) mm and (20, 0, 24) mm. The primary load interface is a flat rectangular pad located on the x-dominant leg region, centered at (66, 12, 0) mm with size 24 mm in x by 22 mm in z. Apply each service load as a distributed load over the load pad with equivalent resultant passing through the load pad center. The bracket must withstand four static load cases: 900 N downward in negative z, 900 N upward in positive z, 450 N in positive x, and 350 N in positive y. Use 6061-T6 aluminum with elastic modulus 69 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.33, yield strength 276 MPa, and density 2700 kg/m^3. Assume machined single-body manufacturing with minimum thickness 4 mm, minimum internal fillet radius 3 mm, and no undercuts. minimum_edge_distance_mm is defined as the minimum distance from hole center to nearest free edge using hole center as the reference point. The objective is to minimize bracket mass while keeping maximum displacement at the load interface below 0.35 mm and maximum von Mises stress below 92.0 MPa under every load case.

evaluation_assumptions.mounting_boundaries: constrained at cylindrical hole surfaces on both mounting planes

reference_solution.notes: Reference solution not yet attached in v1.3 draft. Add a known-feasible baseline CAD/FEA design in a future revision.

feasibility_check.notes: This task is intentionally stiffness-dominated and should be checked with a baseline CAD/FEA design in a future revision.
